passage.title: Energy Saver Board Project
passage.text: The energy saver group wanted children to follow eco jobs more easily. At first, the group posted bright signs that mixed pictures, rules, and reminders together, but important eco steps were hidden inside too much information. Garden helpers said "There are many rules here, and I forget the important part." After hearing that, the team made the most important eco action larger than the rest and spread the directions into smaller sections around the area. They also added one more sign near the compost box so the extra sign repeated the most important action at the last moment. On the next test day, children completed eco jobs more confidently after the redesign. The team noticed that the most important eco message should stand out first. Because of that result, helpers chose to use the same sign ideas for other school eco projects.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: Writing long reports about class rules
Making green jobs easier for children to follow
Painting a room with brighter colors
Buying more books for a library sale
questions.rationale: The whole passage focuses on improving a system for younger students.

questions.prompt: What problem did students mention about the first plan?
choices: Important eco steps were hidden in too much information
The labels were too small to read quickly
The first plan had too many steps
Students could not tell what to do next
questions.rationale: The early part of the passage explains what did not work well.

questions.prompt: What did the helpers set by the door to the recycling corner?
choices: Larger arrows
Short signs
Color blocks
A reminder card
questions.rationale: The middle of the passage tells what the team added or changed.

questions.prompt: What does example mean in the fifth sentence?
choices: A rule about lunch time
A person who leads the school
Something that shows what to do
A room that is easy to clean
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from the way the passage uses it.

questions.prompt: Why did the new design work better for younger students?
choices: It removed every sign from the area
Important eco actions were easier to see first
It made the room much larger
It reduced the number of students
questions.rationale: You have to connect the redesign changes with the later results.

questions.prompt: What will the team most likely do next?
choices: Apply the same sign ideas to other green projects
Use the same clearer style in more places
Remove every sign from the room
Ask students to memorize everything
questions.rationale: The last sentence points to the team's next likely action.
